question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getContractRelatedParent()] Can an unprivileged user craft a hook-triggered freeze transaction where hookParent is non-null, causing getContractRelatedParent() to return hookParent instead of the real parent, so the contract result service processes the freeze under the wrong parent context and the freeze state is applied without the correct authorization chain?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getContractRelatedParent()] Can an unprivileged user submit a child transaction where parent.hasContractResult() is true but the parent is a failed freeze transaction, causing getContractRelatedParent() to return that failed parent and the child unfreeze to be processed under a failed context, permanently leaving the account frozen?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: populateContractTransactions()] Can an unprivileged user trigger a scenario where populateContractTransactions() sets all contractIds on every ContractTransaction to the same shared list, causing a freeze-related contract transaction to be associated with unrelated contract IDs, corrupting the audit trail and making it impossible to identify which accounts were frozen?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: addContractTransaction()] Can an unprivileged user submit a TokenFreeze transaction where addContractTransaction() is called with the frozen account's EntityId but contractTransactionPredicate is null, causing the null-check to skip the entry entirely and the freeze event to be untracked in the contract transaction table, hiding the freeze from monitoring?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getTransactionHash()] Can an unprivileged user submit a TokenFreeze transaction where getEthereumTransaction() returns a non-null EthereumTransaction with a crafted hash, causing getTransactionHash() to return the Ethereum hash instead of the Hedera transaction hash, making the freeze transaction undetectable by hash-based lookups and preventing reversal?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: parseSuccess()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the comment in parseSuccess() ('Consensus nodes have had issues in the past where that invariant did not hold true') by submitting a child TokenFreeze with SUCCESS status whose parent has FAIL_INVALID status, relying on the mirror node's parent-check to mark the child as failed and skip the freeze, but then the freeze is still applied on-chain, creating a divergence where the mirror node shows no freeze but funds are actually frozen?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: parseSuccess()] Can an unprivileged user submit a TokenUnfreeze as a child of a parent with SUCCESS_BUT_MISSING_EXPECTED_OPERATION status, where parseSuccess() returns true for the parent but the missing operation was the actual unfreeze, causing the mirror node to record the unfreeze as successful while the account remains frozen on-chain?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: isChild()] Can an unprivileged user submit a TokenFreeze transaction with a fabricated parentConsensusTimestamp in the record, causing isChild() to return true and the freeze to be treated as a child transaction, bypassing top-level processing guards and applying the freeze without proper validation?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: isChild()] Can an unprivileged user submit a TokenUnfreeze transaction without a parentConsensusTimestamp so isChild() returns false, but the transaction is actually a child in the consensus stream, causing the mirror node to process it as top-level and apply incorrect success logic, potentially skipping the unfreeze and leaving funds frozen?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: parseParent()] Can an unprivileged user submit a batch transaction where a TokenFreeze inner transaction's parentConsensusTimestamp matches previous.parent.transactionRecord.getParentConsensusTimestamp() (grandparent path at line 340-344), causing parseParent() to resolve a null grandparent (previous.parent.parent) and the freeze to be processed without a valid parent context, applying the freeze unconditionally?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: parseParent()] Can an unprivileged user craft a transaction stream where previous is null but the transactionRecord has a parentConsensusTimestamp, causing parseParent() to fall through to return this.parent (which may be null), and the child TokenFreeze to be processed as if it has no parent, bypassing the parent-failure propagation and applying the freeze even when the parent failed?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: parsePossiblyHookContractRelatedParent()] Can an unprivileged user submit a sequence where a TokenFreeze child has a contractCallResult with contractNum == HOOK_CONTRACT_NUM (365), causing parsePossiblyHookContractRelatedParent() to return null (the condition at line 355 excludes HOOK_CONTRACT_NUM), and the freeze to be processed without a hookParent, potentially bypassing hook-based authorization checks?
